name: Regional Finance
description_short: Regional Finance offers personal loans from $1K-$50K for debt consolidation, home/auto repairs, and unexpected expenses, with flexible terms and consideration for less-than-perfect credit.
description_long:
Regional Finance has operated for over 30 years as a personal loan lender with a physical branch presence, including their Houston location at 1804 Wirt Road. The company positions itself as a lending partner rather than a transactional lender, emphasizing relationship-building and personalized service from local branch teams. They serve borrowers across multiple financial situations including debt consolidation, major purchases, home repairs, medical expenses, and unexpected life events.

Regional Finance's core offering includes personal loans alongside secured loan products (auto-secured loans, furniture loans, appliance loans) and specialty financing (holiday loans, travel loans, auto repair loans). They explicitly market their willingness to work with borrowers who have less-than-perfect credit. The company offers flexible payment options and loan insurance products. Their application process includes online prequalification (soft inquiry, no credit impact), document gathering, in-person or phone completion, credit review, and funding conditional on approval.

The Houston branch is managed by Shudney Crumby and operates Monday-Friday 8:30 AM-5:30 PM with Saturday appointments available. Regional Finance's main differentiators are their 30+ year operating history, local branch presence with named managers, explicit acceptance of non-prime borrowers, and multiple loan product types beyond basic personal loans. They offer same-location debt consolidation and loan insurance, suggesting more sophisticated product bundling than basic lenders.

The primary limitation is lack of transparency on their website regarding APR ranges, specific loan amounts, or approval likelihood. While they mention flexible terms, no concrete rate examples or term lengths are disclosed. The reliance on in-person branch interaction or phone completion may deter digital-first borrowers. As a traditional finance company with physical branches, they likely have less streamlined online processes compared to fintech competitors. No explicit statement about credit score minimums or maximum debt-to-income ratios is provided.
pros: 30+ years operating history demonstrates longevity and institutional stability
Willingly serves borrowers with less-than-perfect credit, explicitly stated on website
Multiple loan product types including debt consolidation, auto-secured, furniture, and appliance loans
Free online prequalification with soft credit inquiry (no credit score impact)
Named branch manager (Shudney Crumby) and local Houston presence for in-person service
Extended branch hours Monday-Friday 8:30 AM-5:30 PM plus Saturday appointments
Offers loan insurance products to protect borrowers against payment disruption
cons: Website does not disclose APR ranges, making rate comparison impossible before application
No specific loan amount limits or minimum credit score requirements stated
Requires hard credit pull during approval, which impacts credit score
Limited online application capability—requires in-person branch visit or phone completion
No information provided about approval timeline or average funding speed after approval
best_for: Borrowers with less-than-perfect or damaged credit seeking personalized service and local branch support
Consumers needing debt consolidation with willingness to meet in-person with loan officers
Borrowers in Houston area wanting multi-product financing (appliances, furniture, auto repairs) under one lender
diagnosis: Regional Finance is best for borrowers with challenged credit who value in-person relationship banking and need flexible financing across multiple product categories. The main caveat is complete lack of rate transparency on their website, requiring phone contact or branch visit to understand actual cost of borrowing—making rate shopping difficult.
